entry.title: AVC Procedure Dependency Error When Referencing Variant Table in CU01
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

We would like to manage price variants based on intervals defined in a characteristic. For this purpose, we created an AVC variant table in CU60 with two characteristics: ZLARGEUR_NOMINAL and Y_TEST_PROB_CARACT.

However, when we create an AVC procedure dependency in CU01 using the following editor logic:

 
table Z_PRICE_INTERVAL
( ZLARGEUR_NOMINAL = ZLARGEUR_NOMINAL,
Y_TEST_PROB_CARACT = $self.Y_TEST_PROB_CARACT )

the system displays the following error message:

"Unable to process error message."

Could you please advise what might be causing this issue and how to resolve it?

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: Clean Core – Part 1 – Activation of RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard on Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi Manuel,
You are right, as SCMON and SUSG are run in prod system, it does make sense to execute ATC check in PRD system. At present the values from SCMON & SUSG are Execution of Custom objects (percentage & 3-month) and Unused customer objects (percentage and actual numbers).

If you want to check clean core levels of development that hasn’t yet been moved into Prod, you can do the ATC check in Development/Test. However in this case execution & usage figures do not provide useful information.

entry.title: Result Analysis - Final Settlement - TECO Phase
entry.content:
Greetings everyone,

I'm working on 3 different Result Analysis Methods (Rev Base and Cost Based POC and Complete Contract Method) and currently until the cancellation phase and even with the cancellation of all RA postings I'm fine with all of them. I'm just having an issue or maybe a doubt around my expectation with sending the primary account balances at the WBS Element level which are the GL accounts that has been defined in the OKG5. I'm not able to settle these values like in a normal non RA Related project scenario at the end of the project which I was expecting it to be that way once I get a RA WBS to TECO. I would expect reversing every RA posting which is happening this is fine but the actual values like COGS from delivery or a Manuel Customer Invoice Entry's GL accounts that are defined in the OKG5 just sits there at the end of project (For Complete Contract it is sending them with internal settlement Secondary accounts which I have linked with OKG4 GLs in the okao but it is still doesn't really makes sense to me since it is still settling with secondary cost elements even project is finished). I would expect the primary ones being settled with primary ones at the end of the project once we take the WBS to TECO to wherever we would like to settle just like a normal non-RA project scenario. In the Cost based and Revenue Based it is not even settling anything once WBS is tech. It is just reversing everything.

I just wanted to ask from your experiences if RA designed this way or am I missing some config?


Thank you so much and best wishes.

entry.title: BiWhy – BOBJ Cleanup: Reducing BIAR Export from 1.5 TB to 9 GB
entry.content:
Disclaimer:
BiWhy is available again for FREE to everyone on biwhy.net

The most important step before migrating a SAP BusinessObjects system is cleanup. It can reduce repository size by orders of magnitude and make migrations significantly faster and safer.

Here is one real case example.

Quick win

 BeforeAfter
CMS objects232k50k
Repository size503 GB11 GB
BIAR export estimate~1.5 TB9 GB
Export timeimpractical75 sec

The Problem

During migration preparation, a full system export was attempted using Promotion Management.

The wizard estimated the export size at ~1.5 TB.

Multi-phase promotion is possible, but for small and medium systems a Full Content Promotion is usually simpler and more reliable.

Retention limits are not always helpful because:

  • limits trigger only after the next scheduled run
  • some reports no longer have active schedules
  • limits do not apply to files copied into user folders

Initial System State

CMS Repository

  • CMS objects: 232k
  • Files: 184k
  • Size: 503 GB

TXT / Excel Instances

  • Objects: 34k
  • Files: 31k
  • Size: 479 GB

Platform Search Delta Index

  • Files: 149k
  • Size: 14 GB

Solution

Instances older than one week were removed using BiWhy.

The process is simple.

1. Load instances

Run a CMS query, for example:

SELECT SI_ID, SI_NAME
FROM CI_INFOOBJECTS
WHERE SI_INSTANCE = 1
AND SI_KIND IN ('Txt','Excel')
AND SI_UPDATE_TS < '2026.03.01'

2. Review results

Objects returned by the query can be inspected directly in the repository tree.

3. Delete instances

BiWhy allows deletion:

  • individually
  • recursively inside selected folders
  • all selected objects at once

NB: Because BiWhy allows deletion of general object types, always test the procedure in a non-production environment first.

During testing, use SELECT * to inspect all properties of a sample object you plan to delete.

Important Detail – Recurring Instances

Recurring objects:

  • have modified timestamp = initial creation time
  • use the same SI_KIND as the delivery format

For example, we do not expect Excel (SI_KIND='Excel') to represent a schedule – but it can.

To avoid deleting them, apply additional filters:

SI_RECURRING
SI_SCHEDULE_STATUS

SI_SCHEDULE_STATUS values:

  • COMPLETE = 1
  • EXPIRED = 13
  • FAILURE = 3
  • PAUSED = 8
  • PENDING = 9
  • RUNNING = 0
  • WARNING = 14

Platform Search Cleanup

Clean up Platform Search delta index files by performing a search index rebuild.

Before cleanup

After cleanup

Context delete action

entry.title: Introducing SAP Cloud ERP Private, Nonproduction Usage: A Flexible Path to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Many SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) cloud customers face a necessary upgrade because of upcoming end-of-support deadlines for SAP ERP 6.0 private cloud systems on Enhancement Packages 6-8 (mainstream maintenance ending in 2027 and extended maintenance in 2030).

SAP ECC (SAP ECC 6.0 with Enhancement Package 6 to 8 ) will move to Customer Specific Maintenance starting 1 January 2031. This also means that the SAP ERP, private edition offering will effectively have an end of availability on 31 December 2030. Because of this, many customers will face a time-sensitive transition period. During this time, they may need to run both their existing SAP ERP 6.0 system and the new SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud system in parallel—especially for non-production environments like testing, development, and QA during migration.

Having both environments available can accelerate transformation initiatives, allowing teams to leverage bundled cloud components for innovation, testing, process redesign, and early adoption of new capabilities while the legacy system continues to support ongoing operations.

At the same time, customers seeking a more cost-optimized transition path now have a new option that provides earlier access to a non-production environment without requiring a full parallel subscription. This gives customers greater flexibility to plan and execute their transformation while managing overall subscription costs.

To support this approach, we are introducing SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage. This offering enables unlimited non-production use of SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud during the transition period, allowing customers to perform migration and testing activities without incurring the cost of a full production subscription.

How this helps, for example.

Explanation:

  • Without SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage 
    In Year 2, the SAP ERP, private edition subscription is needed for the production systems, but as non-productive environments are gradually upgraded to the new S/4HANA Private Cloud version, they also need SAP Cloud ERP Private subscription. This overlap leads to simultaneous subscription costs of both SAP ERP, private edition and new SAP Cloud ERP Private systems. 
  • With SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage 
    In Year 2, production continues under the old SAP ERP, private edition subscription, while the non-productive environments are covered by the new SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage product. SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage can be applied to an unlimited number of non-productive systems, allowing full coverage without triggering a full double subscription. In Year 3, all environments have migrated and can now be covered with the normal SAP Cloud ERP Private subscription.

Benefits of SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage  

  • Removes simultaneous subscriptions during the transition phase 
  • Supports testing and validation without the need for exact timing of the switch to a new subscription 
  • Access to the SAP AI portfolio in the nonproduction environment, not available with SAP ECC [SAP ERP 6.0]
  • You only pay for limited support for the non-productive systems during the migration period
  • Covers an unlimited number of non-productive systems 
  • Gives customers the flexibility to plan and execute the migration properly
  • The transition period can be as long as needed, keeping the end of support date in mind.
  • SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage is an addition to the SAP ERP, private edition subscription, extending the product support to S/4HANA Private Cloud in the transition phase. 
  • Helps organizations meet upgrade deadlines without budget overruns

entry.title: Enable Extensibility of Data Determination in Access
entry.content:
Data Determination in the Access enables customers to determine data not available in the document and use it for the accesses in Pricing.

With the extensibility for the structure KOMPAZD , you can create custom fields, determine their values during pricing, and use them in broader scenarios.

Use Cases

Use Case 1

Products in some sales areas have discounts based on variant configurations:

Sales Organization

Distribution Channel

2411

00

AVC_LARGEFORK

1%

AVC_CWEIGHT

2%

 

Without Data Determination in Access, the required condition records are:

Distribution Channel

Variant Condition

VC Discount

AVC_LARGEFORK

1%

1710

00

AVC_CWEIGHT

2%

00

1%

2411

00

AVC_CWEIGHT

2%

 

With Data Determination in Access and its extensibility, you can create a custom field to determine the discount list, and obtain the discount based on the determined list. This reduces the required condition records to:

Sales Organization

1710

1710

AVC_CWEIGHT

2%

Use Case 2

You want to offer discounts on some products (for example, TG11) during promotional season without modifying its material pricing group (for example, A1) in the material master.

1. Create a condition table for data determination to change the Material Pricing Group for TG11. The records can be created as follow.

Material

Material Pricing Group

TG11

A3

2. Use the newly created condition table as an access step in the access sequence. Note that this access step must be placed before the access step for determining prices (the existing condition table below)

Material Pricing Group

Discount

A1

10%

A2

5%

A3

7%

The overall records in the access sequence will be like:

Material

Material Pricing Group

TG11

A3

 

Material Pricing Group

Discount

A1

10%

A2

5%

A3

7%

 

Implementation Steps with Example and Screenshots Example

 In sales organization 1710 and distribution channel 10, customer buy a forklift which is a configuration material, if customer select the large fork in the variant configuration, they can get 1% additional discount.

  1. Create a Custom Field for Surcharge Price List in KOMPAZD and enable related configurations.
  2. Add the Custom Field in Field Category
  3. Create a condition table to determine Surcharge Price List
  4. Create a condition table to retrieve surcharge price based on Surcharge Price List
  5. Use the two condition tables in Access Sequence.

To simplify the steps, we will simplify the example as follow:

Sales Organization

Distribution Channel

Variant Condition

Discount

1710

10

AVC_LARGEFORK

1%

The related Data Determination Table and records are as follow:

Sales Organization

Distribution Channel

Discount Group

1710

10

001

The related Discount Table and records are as follow:

Discount Group

Variant Condition

Discount

001

AVC_LARGEFORK

1%

We will give screenshots for the following:

  1. Create a custom field based on Extensibility of Data Determination in Access. The label will be set as KX_DIS_GRP, and it will work as Discount Group Field.
  2. Configure the condition tables for data determination and discount, as well as the pricing procedure.
  3. Test.

Create Custom Field

To create a custom field, open the Custom Fields app and click the ‘+’ button.

Next, select SD_PRICEDATADETN as the Business Context. Enter a label (no more than 14 characters) for your custom field. The Identifier and tooltip will be auto-generated.

If you plan to use this custom field in the Condition Record UI or through APIs, enable the usage accordingly. Finally, save and publish your custom field.

Configure Pricing

Change Field Catalog

In your configuration environment, use the search function to open the configuration activity ‘Change Field Catalog for Pricing in Sales’. If you're using the "Configure Your Solution" app, you can refer to the configuration ID 103118.

You can add the Custom Field by clicking New Entries and choosing the Field you created previously. You can refer to the following two images.

Click the New Entries Button, then choose the Custom Field you need to use in your procedure and click the Save button. In this case, we use YY1_KX_DIS_GRP_PDC. Prefix YY1_ and suffix _PDC are added automatically when you create the Custom Field.

Create Condition Tables

At least two condition tables need to be created.

Data Determination Table

This table will be used to map Sales Area to related Discount Groups:

Sales Organization

Distribution Channel

KX_DIS_GRP

1710

10

001

The Field KX_DIS_GRP will work as Discount Group field we mentioned previously.

Price Table

This table will get price based on Discount Group determined by Data Determination Table and Condition Variant.:

KX_DIS_GRP

Variant Condition

Discount

001

AVC_LARGEFORK

1%

Data Determination Table

In your configuration environment, use the search function to open the configuration activity ‘Create Condition Tables for Pricing in Sales’. If you're using the "Configure Your Solution" app, you can refer to the configuration ID 103119.

In this case, you add three fields: ‘Sales Organization’, ‘Distribution Channel’, and ‘KX_DIS_GRP’. ‘KX_DIS_GRP’ is the Custom Field in KOMAZD to determine Discount Group.

In Technical View, the Key and Text Field flag should be removed for KX_DIS_GRP, you can refer to the following screenshot. After removing the flags, click the Generate button.

Price Table

The Price Table will determine the price based on KX_DIS_GRP, so KX_DIS_GRP will be the only Key Field in the Price Table. The Price Table will be created as follows:

Use Created Tables in Access

Table 9AE is only used to map Sales Area and Discount Group. Therefore, to avoid it showing up in conditions and influencing the calculation, the ‘Data Determination Only’ flag should be enabled for the Condition Table.

To avoid the value of KX_DIS_GRP being used across different Variant, we use step 110 to initialize the value of KX_DIS_GRP.

To make YY1_KX_DIS_GRP storable in step 120 and obtainable in step 130, the value should be set to and retrieved from a field in KOMPAZD. Here we choose KOMPAZD-YY1_KX_DIS_GRP_PCD as an example. This field will be created after the Custom Field created in the previous step is published successfully. To implement the logic, you can refer to the following screenshots.

 Then you can use the Custom Field in KOMAZD in your procedure after the following:

  • Assing the Access Sequence with the Condition Type.
  • Add the Condition Type in the Procedure you decide to use.
  • Set Procedure Determination correctly.
  • Add condition records.

To implement these steps, you can refer to the following screenshots.

Assign the Access Sequence with the Condition Type.

Add the Condition Type in the Procedure you decide to use.

Set Pricing Procedure Determination.

 

Create condition records.

Test

In step 110, KX_DIS_GRP will be initialized to 000 and stored in KOMPAZD. Then In Step 120, KX_DIS_GRP will be set to 001. Because steps 110 and 120 are set to ‘Data Determination Only’, they will not show up as price conditions. However, we can check this in the analysis.

 

Related Links:

  • Data Determination in Access (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/cd8cc95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html?)
  • Example for Data Determination in Access (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/d08cc95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html?)
  • Extensibility of Data Determination in Access (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/f87c82cbb63e4d24a5754831c16d81e3.html?state=DRAFT)

entry.title: Re: Can Custom Alternative Material Logic Be Embedded in...
entry.content:
Thank you very much for your detailed response—it has clearly guided our approach to advising the customer.
However, I still have two key questions that I’d like to confirm:
  1. Flexibility of the BAdI in advanced usage:
    When using the PPH_SUPPLY_DEMAND_LIST BAdI intensively, can it support highly customized logic?
    For example, during an MRP run, could we dynamically modify the BOM components and their required quantities for a specific product based on custom rules?
    A concrete scenario:
    • Product A’s standard BOM includes materials B and C, each with a requirement of 10 units.
    • During MRP execution, I would like to temporarily adjust the available stock of material B to 10 (so no procurement proposal is generated),
      and simultaneously replace the requirement for material C with requirements for materials D and E (each with 5 units of available stock),
      thereby ensuring that no procurement or production proposals are created for B or C.
    Is this kind of “runtime BOM substitution” logic achievable via this BAdI?
  2. Appropriate usage boundaries in a Public Cloud context:
    Given that SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition strictly adheres to the Clean Core principle, any modification to core logic may introduce upgrade risks or architectural contamination.
    Therefore, in what scenarios would the use of this BAdI be considered appropriate and safe in a Public Cloud environment?
    Are there official best practices or constraints that ensure such extensions meet business needs while remaining compliant with cloud-native governance principles?
We greatly appreciate your further guidance. Thank you!

entry.title: Default Partner Bank and Payment methods from Supplier Master to Invoice
entry.content:
Hi Team,

The business requires the ability to default the payment method and partner bank details on the invoice based on the invoice currency maintained in the Supplier Master.

  • Currently, a single payment method is maintained in the Supplier Master, and we want this to be automatically populated on the invoice by default.

  • Additionally, when the Supplier Master contains multiple bank accounts, the system should automatically select the appropriate bank account based on the invoice currency.

Kindly advise on the available solution in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to enable auto-population of these fields from master data into the invoice during invoice posting.

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial
Auto create Ship to party while creating the sales order
entry.content:
Hi All,

The business requirement is to enable creation of a Ship-To Party directly during sales order creation in SAP SD (S/4HANA) without navigating to the Business Partner (BP) transaction.

A checkbox should be added on the sales order screen, such as “Create Ship-To” or “Save as Ship-To.”

Proposed logic:

The system should first check whether the entered address already exists for the customer.

If the address does not exist, the system should create a new Ship-To Party and automatically assign it to the respective Sold-To Party during sales order creation or when the sales order is saved.

 

Please suggest how to proceed.

Thank you.

entry.title: Unlocking Transformational Value at SAP Sapphire 2026 Orlando
entry.content:
Unlocking Transformational Value at SAP Sapphire: Join the Exclusive ASUG Pre-Conference on the New SAP Services & Support Portfolio

SAP is entering a new era of services and support—one that is simpler, more intelligent, and designed to help organizations move faster toward cloud transformation and AI-driven innovation. To help customers understand and fully leverage this new approach, ASUG and SAP are hosting an exclusive full-day pre-conference at SAP Sapphire:

“Unlocking Transformational Value: A Practical Introduction to the New SAP Services & Support Portfolio.”

This immersive event brings together SAP leaders, product experts, and customers to walk through the modernized SAP Services & Support portfolio, explain the new tiered Success Plans, and demonstrate how organizations can accelerate cloud adoption, improve operations, and drive measurable business outcomes. With breakfast, lunch, expert sessions, and an exclusive showfloor tour, the day provides both strategic insight and practical guidance for anyone responsible for SAP transformation. Whether you are planning a cloud migration, optimizing hybrid landscapes, or exploring how AI can transform operations, this session will help you understand how to maximize value from your SAP investment.
Register Now!

What You’ll Learn

During this full-day experience, participants will explore:

  • The new SAP Success Plans: Foundational, Advanced, and Max

  • How AI-enabled services improve productivity and automation

  • Ways to optimize SAP operations and governance

  • How Development Services and co-innovation accelerate innovation

  • How Application Management and Sovereign Cloud support modern enterprise requirements

You’ll leave with practical guidance for building your SAP success strategy for 2026 and beyond. Register Now!

Full Agenda May 11 | Orlando

8:30–9:00 AM — Welcome

Welcome & Setting the Stage

Speakers:
Geoff Scott, CEO and Chief Community Champion, ASUG
Thomas Saueressig, Member of the Executive Board of SAP SE, Head of Customer Services & Delivery

Max for Strategic Partnership and Business-Critical Success

Explore SAP’s highest level of engagement with the Max Success Plan. Learn how dedicated experts, proactive governance, AI-driven insights, and mission-critical operations support help enterprises protect business continuity, accelerate innovation, and drive strategic outcomes at scale.

1:30–2:15 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Co-Innovating with SAP Development Services: From AI Ideas to Impact

Sindhu Gangadharan, Head, Customer Innovation Services; MD, SAP Labs India

Discover how SAP Development Services partners with customers to build tailored solutions—from generative AI use cases to agentic AI scenarios. This session includes a real-world example demonstrating how custom AI innovation improved efficiency, decision-making, and measurable business outcomes.

2:15–3:00 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Next Generation Application Management for Predictive, Intelligent, and Automated Operations

Lalit Patil, EVP & Chief Technology Officer, SAP
Vinay Hiriganahalli, SVP, Head of Cloud Application Services, SAP

Learn how SAP Application Management is transforming operations from reactive support to predictive, AI-driven optimization. Through artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cognitive automation, organizations can move beyond issue resolution toward proactive performance management and intelligent operations.

3:00–3:15 PM — Break

3:15–3:45 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Secure, Compliant Innovation for Regulated Industries and Public Sector

Peter Keller, VP, Sovereign Cloud Architecture, SAP

Explore how SAP Sovereign Cloud helps organizations meet strict data residency, compliance, and security requirements while still accelerating innovation. The session covers architecture, use cases, and practical steps for adopting sovereign cloud models across regulated industries and the public sector.

3:45–4:30 PM — Strategy Session

Build Your SAP Success Strategy: Practical Guidance and Roadmap 2026

entry.title: Re: Auto create Ship to party while creating the sales order
entry.content:
Ship-To Party creation should remain in the Business Partner (BP) transaction to ensure proper validations, approvals, and master data governance.

Alternative approach would be allow users to enter a “one-time Ship-To address” directly in the sales order (SAP standard supports this). If the address is frequently reused, then create a proper Ship-To BP via master data processes. If business insists, you could design a controlled workflow where the checkbox triggers a background process to request Ship-To creation, routed through master data governance (MDG) or a workflow approval, rather than creating it instantly.

In short, don’t club master data creation with transactional processes. Instead, leverage SAP’s standard one-time address functionality or build a controlled workflow to balance speed with governance.

entry.title: Issues with Data Analyzer Saved Views & Missing Save As Option in New Quick Builder
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We recently enabled both Data Analyzer and the new Quick Builder features in SAP Analytics Cloud, and our end users are facing two issues. I would appreciate guidance or best practices from the community.

1. Data Analyzer – Saved Insight View Not Reopening Properly

We enabled:

  • Data Analyzer option for Tables
  • Security role → Create permission for users

Users are able to: ✔️ Open Data Analyzer
✔️ Create their insight view
✔️ Save the view locally

However, when they reopen the saved Data Analyzer view, SAC shows errors and warnings, and the previously selected filters/slices are not visible.

Questions:

  • How can we prevent these errors/warnings when reopening a saved Data Analyzer view?
  • What configuration is required so that the user’s previously saved view (filters, layout, selections) loads correctly?
  • Are there any model, permission, or data access dependencies we should check?

    2. New Quick Builder – “Save As” Option Not Available

    When users analyze data using the New Quick Builder, they see only the Export option.

    After performing slice‑and‑dice analysis, they do not get a “Save As” option.

    Questions:

    • How can we enable the Save As option for users in the new Quick Builder?
    • Is there a specific role/privilege required to save a quick‑built view?
    • Once saved, should the view be retrievable from their local folder (similar to classic Data Analyzer)? If yes, what setup is needed?

    Best Regards,

    Govardhan

entry.title: Re: How to do a Material Master assessment
entry.content:
To answer your question "Where to Start MM Assessment", perform the below steps

1. Inventory of Material Masters
Identify active vs. inactive materials (materials not used in transactions for years). Classify by product type (raw materials, semi-finished, finished goods, trading goods, etc.). Check plant-specific views — some materials may exist globally but not be relevant at certain plants.

2. Data Quality Checks
Mandatory fields: Ensure base unit of measure, material type, valuation class, procurement type are filled correctly.
Consistency: Align descriptions, units of measure, and material groups.
Duplicates: Identify and merge/remove duplicate material codes.

3. MRP-Relevant Fields
Procurement type (in-house vs. external), MRP type (PD, VB, ND, etc.), Lot-sizing procedure, Lead times (planned delivery, GR processing, in-house production) & Safety stock and reorder point settings.

4. Supplier & Purchasing Data
Source lists and info records. Valid purchasing groups and vendor assignments. Payment terms and pricing conditions.

5. Plant & Storage Data
Storage locations, warehouse management views. Stock levels vs. material status (obsolete, blocked, discontinued).

So the recommended approach would be

Baseline Assessment
Run reports to list all materials by plant, type, and usage. Flag inactive or obsolete materials.

Data Cleansing
Archive or block unused materials. Standardize descriptions, units, and groups. Correct missing or inconsistent MRP fields.

Governance Setup
Define clear rules for material creation (mandatory fields, naming conventions). Assign ownership (who approves new materials).

MRP Readiness
Once master data is clean, configure MRP parameters. Pilot MRP with a subset of materials before rolling out broadly.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition：为什么越来越多的客户开始使用 Parallel Line（并行线）？
entry.content:
随着企业对业务灵活性、快速创新与高质量上线的要求不断提升，SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition（SAP ERP公有云）在 2025 年正式引入了 Parallel Line（并行线） 的概念。这一能力自发布以来，迅速成为客户最关注的创新之一。截止作者发布此文为止，已有超过200家客户激活并开始使用了并行线功能。

         那么，“并行线”到底是什么？为什么企业迫切需要它？它能解决哪些痛点？希望这篇文章将带你完整理解其价值。

一、为什么需要 Parallel Line？——来自客户的真实声音

         长期以来，SAP ERP公有云的3系统架构（开发、测试、生产）虽然能满足核心实施项目的需要，但在企业实际操作中，经常出现以下诉求：

  • 想要单独的 数据迁移模拟环境
  • 想要并行 SIT/UAT 测试环境
  • 想要 长期稳定培训环境
  • 想要验证 新的第三方集成或新功能
  • 想要为 新增国家、组织结构提供独立配置空间
  • 想让 后续项目不影响当前主线或生产环境

         这些诉求在传统3系统架构中无法完全满足。

         因此，Parallel Line 并行线诞生了。

二、什么是 Parallel Line？

         Parallel Line（以下简称 PL）可以理解为：

“基于主线复制出的第二套配置与测试环境，用于扩展项目、迁移演练和集成测试，并且可选择将配置合并回主线。”

         并行线由以下组成：

  • CBC 内给PL单独的工作区（workspace）
  • 开发系统的并行定制租户（D120）
  • 测试系统的并行测试租户（T120）

         它是一条与主线平行存在的“支线”。

📘 并行线与主线的关系（图示）

三、Parallel Line 并行线能做什么？——典型应用场景

PL 的应用场景可分为两大类：

💡（A）仅需要 Branch（不需合并回主线）

这些场景仅需要复制出并行线，然后单独使用即可，例如：

  • 构建 多轮数据迁移测试
  • 多场景测试：SIT、UAT、回归测试
  • 构建第三方 集成测试环境
  • 提供长期稳定的 培训环境
  • 展示新功能、新的Scope Item等

以下是简化场景矩阵：

💡（B）使用PL后需要合并回主线

这些场景是指在PL并行线中完成相关的操作，然后把所做的更改合并回主线，例如：

  • 添加 新国家/地区
  • 扩展 新的组织架构
  • 增加新的范围项目（Scope item）
  • 增强产品特定配置

 以下是简化场景矩阵：

 

四、使用 Parallel Line并行线 的六大核心好处

 

1. 额外、独立的迁移 / 测试 / 培训环境

避免资源冲突，解决：

  • 测试之间互相覆盖
  • 培训占用测试环境
  • 数据迁移无法反复演练

2. 扩展性配置不影响主线/生产

例如：

  • 新增国家/地区
  • 新公司代码、工厂
  • 新本地化流程
  • 扩展 范围项目（Scope Item）

可以全部在 PL 中进行。

3. 提高数据迁移成功率

因为PL并行线环境可以反复刷新，所以数据迁移可在并行线环境中反复演练，保证上线质量。

4. 更强的集成验证能力

PL 是一套独立并且完整的系统，包含了除生产机之外的整套系统架构，可做：

  • SAP 内部集成验证
  • 第三方接口验证
  • 中间件流程测试

5. 稳定培训环境

         也有不少客户需要给新员工提供一个培训环境，PL并行线不影响生产、不被当前测试影响、同时也会跟随版本升级而升级，是一个作为培训的最佳环境。

**6. 最重要：**无需额外订阅费用

PL并行线环境包含在了客户3系统云订阅中，无需额外付费。客户可在SAP for Me里自助激活。

激活流程大致如下：

1. 在SAP for Me里，选择Service & Support，点击New Service Request：

 2. 选择Partner & Other Cloud Services，选择Create Service Request：

 3. 在Service Catalog中输入关键字Parallel，会找到并行线相关服务。Check Prerequisites服务用来检查是否有创建并行线的前提条件，Branch服务用来创建并行线。请首先选择Check Prerequisites服务，通过之后选择Branch服务：

 

五、并行线 vs 沙箱：业务场景差异

         在与一些客户的交流中，经常有一些客户会问到并行线系统与沙箱（sandbox）或者starter系统的区别，现把两者的区别整理如下：

 

一句话总结：

沙箱用于“看”，并行线用于“干”。

六、为什么并行线值得企业采用？

从业务管理层角度看，并行线的最大价值在于：

  • ✔ 提高上线成功率
  • ✔ 提供快速扩展能力
  • ✔ 降低创新风险
  • ✔ 分离配置波次，保证稳定性
  • ✔ 支撑全球化、多国家 rollout

来自使用过并行线功能的真实客户评价：

“我们真诚地认为，并行线环境已经，并且将继续对 SAP 项目的成功产生重大影响。”

“我们将并行线视为非常重要的创新，对未来新的实施至关重要。如果我们没有并行线，生产租户将面临风险。”

七、结语

         Parallel Line 并行线已经成为 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中最具战略价值的能力之一。无论是数据迁移、测试、培训，还是复杂的扩展配置，它都带来了前所未有的灵活性与安全性。

         如果你的企业正在：

  • 推全球化 rollout
  • 多批次上线
  • 多系统集成
  • 有复杂迁移场景

         并行线几乎是必备能力。

         如果您看了这篇文章，想了解更多关于Parallel Line并行线的内容，我也把几个非常有用的文档链接分享如下：

  • Parallel Line Overview文档
  • 并行线SAP Help Portal链接
  • Parallel Line FAQ

         当然，有任何问题也可以与我联系。感谢阅读！

entry.title: Re: SAP Field Logistics: Next Generation Kitting & Staging for MRO Industries
entry.content:
Thank you for responding, @simon_thomson99 

Ok, if a business has only implemented IM (has not implemented EWM or any other WMS) visibility of each material from the moment it leaves its bin is the problem.  When I was managing several warehouses for a very large mining company in Australia my core problem was the need for multiple bin locations, so I looked to WMS (at the time) to fix this, but I did not need or want all the additional functionality that came with it.  So, my question to you is how does Field Logistics (FL) provide the visibility of the individual material from the bin, then to the kit, then to the staging? 

If a business is only using IM, the material needs be issued out of stock once it's removed from the bin.  How does FL provide visibility of the issued and kitted materials to the staging point?  Visibility is the core need, which is what was pushing me to EWM but this functionality is a considerable lift in transactional workload in mining environment.

Cheers,

Tim

entry.title: Integrating SAP Databricks with SAP CPQ and SAP Datasphere for Analytics & Reporting
entry.content:
In today’s data-driven enterprises, sales teams rely on real-time insights to make faster and smarter decisions. Yet, many organizations still struggle with fragmented data landscapes—especially when dealing with complex systems like SAP CPQ (Configure Price Quote), SAP Datasphere, and advanced analytics platforms.

To solve this challenge, we designed an end‑to‑end architecture that seamlessly connects SAP CPQ, Databricks, and SAP Datasphere. The result? A unified, governed, and scalable analytics ecosystem that unlocks deeper sales insights.

This blog breaks down the business goals, architecture, and implementation blueprint behind the integration.

Birds High View. (Process View)

Why This Integration Matters

The core objective is simple but powerful:
Bring SAP CPQ quotation data into SAP Datasphere—through Databricks—to enable centralized reporting, analytics, and visualization.

This pipeline creates:

  • A single source of truth for CPQ data
  • Real‑time or near‑real‑time insights
  • A governed, secure, and scalable data platform
  • Smooth interoperability across SAP and non‑SAP systems

Business Requirements at a Glance

Functional Requirements

To support modern analytics use cases, the solution must:

  • Extract all sales quotation records from SAP CPQ via APIs
  • Store data in a governed, versioned Delta Lake format
  • Share data securely with SAP Datasphere
  • Enable full data lineage and governance
  • Support real-time or near-real-time use cases

Non‑Functional Requirements

Behind the scenes, the platform must also ensure:

  • Performance: Process incremental CPQ changes with <5‑minute latency
  • Security: Encryption and role‑based access control throughout
  • Scalability: Handle 100,000+ quotations per day
  • Compliance: GDPR and SOX adherence for sensitive financial data

Solution Architecture Overview

The integration spans four major layers: data sources, data platform, data sharing, and data consumption.

1. Data Sources

The journey begins with SAP CPQ, which exposes:

  • REST APIs for extracting quotation data
  • OAuth 2.0 authentication using client credentials

These APIs provide structured access to all quotation objects and line items.

2. Databricks – The Data Transformation Engine

Databricks acts as the data engineering and governance hub.

  • PySpark ETL notebooks orchestrate ingestion and transformation
  • Unity Catalog oversees centralized governance
  • Delta Lake ensures ACID transactions and version-controlled storage

This allows teams to build clean, enriched, analytics-ready datasets.

3. Data Sharing: Delta Sharing + SAP BDC

For secure, open data sharing, the architecture leverages:

  • Delta Sharing Protocol for exchanging datasets using industry standards
  • SAP BDC (Business Data Connectivity) for ORD-based integration into Datasphere

The combination ensures that SAP Datasphere receives fresh, trusted data with minimal friction.

4. Consumption Layer

Finally, business users analyze the curated CPQ data through:

  • SAP Datasphere for modeling and semantic layering
  • SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for dashboards and reports

The result is a seamless analytics experience with governed enterprise data.

The Data Model: What’s Being Shared?

To enable deep sales analytics, the shared data model includes:

Key Entities

  • Quotations: Quote ID, Quote Status, Opportunity ID, Customer ID, Amount, Status, Quote Created Date
  • Line Items: Product ID, Quantity, Unit Price, Net Price, Target Price, Margins
  • Customers: Business Partners,  Region, Segment
  • Products: Product Name, Category, SKU

Relationships

  • Quotation → Line Items: One-to-many
  • Quotation → Customer: Many-to-one

This structure supports reporting such as win rate trends, regional sales patterns, product profitability, and more.

Integration Flows

1. Data Ingestion 

  • Frequency: Hourly incremental loads
  • One Example API Endpoint: /api/v1/quotes
  • Resilience: Retry logic with exponential backoff

2. Data Transformation in Databricks

Transformations include:

  • Cleansing and deduplication
  • Enriching quotations with master data
  • Creating derived metrics like:
    • Win Rate
    • Average Deal Size
    • Quote-to-close duration

3. Data Governance

To meet compliance and governance needs:

  • PII fields are tagged and masked
  • Row-level security applies by sales region
  • All schema changes are version controlled through Delta Lake

Security & Compliance Framework

The pipeline is designed with enterprise-grade security:

  • Authentication: Service principals for API access
  • Authorization: Fine-grained ACLs via Unity Catalog
  • Encryption: TLS for in-transit, AES‑256 for at-rest data
  • Auditability: Complete logging of data access and transformations

This ensures audit readiness for GDPR, SOX, and internal IT security checks.

How We Measure Success

To validate business adoption and technical reliability, we track:

Data Quality

99% accuracy of quotation data

Platform Availability

  • 99.9% uptime SLA for the pipeline

User Adoption

  • 50+ monthly active business users in SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: How does rowNumber() in the Custom Expression Editor of SAC import setting take effect?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to get some acquired data into my SAC model, and my acquired data is sorted by a certain dimension. During the import setting, I need to transform the data based on the original acquired data row number. To better identify the row number, I add [column] = rowNumber() in the Custom Expression Editor. However, the Custom Expression Help says that Row numbers will not be the same between sampled data and full data, or between data refreshes. I am a little concerned whether the row number generated in SAC import setting can truly reflect my sorted original data. 

Has anyone run into a similar issue before?
 
Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance.
 
SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Proactive Cross Plant Planning in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi PM Community,

We are in a situation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition;

Master Data maintained in Technical Object(Equipment)

Planning Plant 1001

Maintenance Work Center is 1003PM

Work Center Plant is 1003

When the Maintenance Item is being created we get this error;


If we change manually the Main Work Center Plant into 1003, then the error message get's fixed and item can be saved.

If we change the Planning Plant for technical object into 1003, then the Main Work Center Plant is 1003 in the Maintenance Item, so it fetches the Main Work Center Plant behind the Planning Plant.

In the configuration Planning Plant for Plant 1003, is 1003.

Is this a bug in system, is there something we should not maintain in the technical object master data or configuration issue?

What would be the solution that the data inside the maintenance item doesn't need to be performed manually?

entry.title: Re: Seeking Configuration Points for Colombia DRC (F350,...
entry.content:
Good day @JuanseVal 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, you must configure Colombia statutory reports that run in DRC with “Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting” in CBC. The ID is 101071 and not 102220. Then you must execute it via Run Statutory Reports (F1515).

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cb9131e59ba34741923bd19ef4cb1f90/fe63969394144a1a8f739a72ee962902.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cb9131e59ba34741923bd19ef4cb1f90/5cba78c307e94aea97941e9069b4713e.html?locale=en-US

For VAT Declaration Form 300, the report category CO_VAT_DCL and the mapping logic based on the Tax Box Framework is provided, meaning you must maintain tax box structure and mappings (SSCUIs 103011, 103012, 103013) in addition to the DRC reporting setup i mentioned above.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cb9131e59ba34741923bd19ef4cb1f90/20b4552d3a994d3c8d49748b2f804319.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0c676015e0414931bdc9ebacd6eb2dff/08b369f208ea47d1bc0ac852798f665f.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cb9131e59ba34741923bd19ef4cb1f90/d85589681d854300806f78b6c40acaf9.html?locale=en-US

For Formato 350, you can generate the report in Run Statutory Reports for Colombia and then maintain the configuration in the same DRC setup activity i mentioned earlier, together with the withholding tax configuration prerequisites. Note that this is not done with the tax box framework.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cb9131e59ba34741923bd19ef4cb1f90/fe63969394144a1a8f739a72ee962902.html?locale=en-US

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3640596

For income tax declaration (Declaracion de Renta), the best practice the public cloud offers is to confirm whether it is delivered in your tenant. Look at Colombia report list in the Report Name value help inside Runs Statutory Reports. If it is not listed there, then it is not available as a standard statutory report in your system.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Elimination of SAP PI PO systems
entry.content:
SAP PI/PO was built for the on‑premise era. SAP Integration Suite — especially Cloud Integration — is designed for a cloud‑native ecosystem. Sooner or later, SAP has to modernize its integration platform to keep up with current technology trends. If they continued relying solely on PI/PO, the product would eventually fall behind.

I understand your frustration — many years of effort, expertise, and best practices built around PI/PO can feel like they’re being lost. But the reality is: your experience is not wasted. While tools evolve, the core principles of integration architecture remain the same. Message modeling, orchestration, error handling, security, governance — these fundamentals still apply.

In the coming years, especially with the acceleration brought by AI, technology will continue to evolve even faster. The best strategy is to keep learning, stay adaptable, and continue building on your foundation. That’s where long‑term success comes from.

entry.title: Tracking Technician Cost in Project Management in SAP Business One Without Affecting Financial Repor
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on a Project Management setup in SAP Business One for an air-conditioning sales and servicing company.

The requirement is to track technician costs against specific Project Stages. One employee may work on multiple sub-projects or stages under a single Financial Project.

If we link technician costs through A/P documents or Journal Entries, it may result in duplicate cost impact in financial reports, since the company already posts the monthly salary through payroll entries.

Therefore, I would like to find an approach to track technician costs per project/stage for internal monitoring purposes only, without affecting financial postings.

What would be the best or simplest method to track technician costs for specific projects or stages in SAP Business One?

Thank you in advance for your advice.

Best Regards,

Khat

entry.title: Re: Invoice delivery audit
entry.content:
Hi,

The first thing that comes to mind, is that when I read you question, it sounds like you want two somewhat contradictory things, because you sometimes keep sold goods in your warehouse, until the customer picks it up:

  1. Make B1 show you stock on the shelf, including quantities that are technically not yours anymore.
  2. Make B1 tell you how much stock you have available for sales.

In the scenario, where the customer picks up goods at a later time, do you keep these goods physically among your normal stock?

How about moving sold goods to a separate shelf or part of your warehouse? That way you can just create the Delivery Note right away. You could print the delivery and keep it with the goods, while the customer gets the invoice receipt. That way you can compare the two. You could also create a user defined field at the document level, where you enter the warehouse location code or shelf number of the shelf where you put the sold goods.

Regards,

Johan

Unfortunately, this feature is not yet supported due to the requirement for a cloud connector. However, this limitation (which is planned to be removed in the future) can be bypassed by adding a HANA Cloud instance to act as a proxy: BTP ABAP → HANA Cloud → S/4 Private Cloud.

For more information, please refer to the following resources:

Accessing ABAP-Managed Data from SAP HANA Cloud and SAP HANA | SAP Help PortalCreate an ABAP On-Premise Remote Source | SAP Help Portal

Leon

entry.title: AI-based evaluation for requirement approvals in SAP Cloud ALM with external workflows in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Objectives

This SAP blog post explains how to improve requirement approvals by combining AI-assisted pre-evaluation in SAP Cloud ALM with policy-controlled, multi-step approval workflows in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BPA). New requirements are assessed against three ISO/IEC 25010 software product quality criteria: functionality, completeness and correctness. The classification and score of the requirement's quality will be automatically evaluated by an AI model. 

The handover to SAP BPA enables dynamic approver determination, SLA handling, escalations, and two- or four-eyes principles for sensitive changes, including multiple approval workflows. End-to-end traceability remains available in SAP Cloud ALM, where teams can review the approval history; the AI rationale is accessible in SAP BPA. 

Important Note: This is a solution pattern based on SAP Consulting Best Practices. It is not provided out of the box. You need to set it up (for example: configure Cloud ALM subscriptions/webhooks, a BPA process, and the AI Launchpad integration) before using it in your projects.

Prerequisites

  • Please review my previous SAP blog post to implement necessary configurations, because the AI workflow will be build on that solution process.
  • You have additionally provisioned an SAP AI Core service, which is running in your SAP BTP system landscape. A service key for the instance is created as well. The AI Launchpad is mandatory to control and govern the consumption, while the SAP AI Core is the underlying engine, which executes the LLMs. The setup can be configured on the SAP help page.

Procedure Overview

  1. AI workflow architecture and end-to-end flow.
  2. AI entity model (data and decision objects).
  3. Technical setup and configurations.
    3.1. Orchestration v2 API action in SAP BPA.
    3.2. Bidirectional communication via destinations.
    3.3. Enhanced solution process workflow.
  4. Result.
  5. Conclusion.

1. AI workflow architecture process

In SAP Cloud ALM, the project is the top-level container. Each requirement is the item that needs evaluation and approval, that carries the functional context to drive policy checks and human review later. For event-driven integration, the project uses a subscription which is the secured REST endpoint and exposed by SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BPA). The subscription points to a webhook and to a mapping that transforms the payloads into a schema which is expected by SAP BPA and the SAP AI Core service.

In SAP BPA, a project provides the artifacts needed to run the approval workflow. The process, as the central part of the project, coordinates system steps and tasks, applies business rules and thresholds, and manages SLAs and escalations. The process uses automations for technical work and actions for tasks such as data enrichment or standard REST integrations to AI Core or back to SAP Cloud ALM. It also includes forms to collect input and decisions from approvers in two- or four-eyes reviews. Events from SAP Cloud ALM start or update process instances, and SAP BPA stores the AI evaluation and decision history for audits.

In SAP AI Core, the workspace is the operational area for AI assets and configurations used by the workflow. The deployment exposes an inference endpoint of the chosen AI model used for the requirement evaluation. Optionally, Orchestration pipelines can implement pre- and post-processing around the model call - for example, prompt construction, payload normalization and output formatting. SAP BPA calls the deployment via REST, receives the structured evaluation (including scoring and rationale per criterion), and uses them to auto-approve, send to human review, or reject.

2. AI entity model

This AI entity model connects SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BPA), and SAP AI Core on SAP BTP to deliver AI-assisted requirement approvals end to end. The flow starts in SAP Cloud ALM when a requirement enters an approval workflow. SAP Cloud ALM starts a workflow event and hands over the requirement context through a secured external API to SAP BPA via REST. SAP BPA acts as the orchestration layer and enriches the payload with project and task metadata, then calls an inference REST endpoint in SAP AI Core to request an AI evaluation.

In SAP AI Core, we execute the evaluation by a configured Gemini model. The model assesses the requirement against  three ISO/IEC 25010 quality characteristics and returns structured outputs such as criterion-level scores, an overall risk/quality score, and an explainable rationale. These AI results flow back to SAP BPA, where decision logic and business policies are applied. Depending on thresholds and risk levels, SAP BPA routes the requirement to human approvers (two- or four-eyes). Both, the AI rationale and scoring matrix are captured as process artifacts and can remain audit-ready.

Once a decision is reached, SAP BPA posts the outcome back to SAP Cloud ALM through a transactional API. SAP Cloud ALM updates the workflow state (approved or rejected) and attaches the evaluation evidence to the requirement in SAP BPA.

3. Technical setup configurations

3.1. SAP BTP destination

Before we can use the Orchestration API of SAP AI Launchpad, it is necessary to build up the SAP BTP destination. Navigate to your SAP BTP subaccount, where you have provisioned your SAP BPA instance tenant and create a new destination. Ensure to add at least the following details:

  • Name of your BTP destination.
  • Type: HTTP.
  • Proxy Type: Internet.
  • Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials.
  • URL: << url of your Service API URL from the service key >>.
  • Client ID: << client id of your service key >>.
  • Client Secret: << client secret of your service key >>.
  • Token Service URL: << URL /oauth/token >> .
  • Additional Properties:
    • URL.headers.Content-Type: application/json
    • URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group: default

3.2. Orchestration API

Back in SAP BPA, go to the Actions which allows us to connect the workflow with the external AI Launchpad.

Please choose the correct API source from SAP Business Accelerator Hub and select the newest Orchestration API v2:

To invoke the Gemini model from SAP Build Process Automation, we call the SAP AI Core Orchestration Completion API via the BTP destination (with OAuth2 Client Credentials). We use a deployment-specific endpoint: POST /v2/inference/deployments/{deploymentId}. In the request, we provide a minimal configuration with prompt templating (the message to the model) and the model binding (the deployed Gemini version). 

Here is a minimal payload example based on Orchestration v2.

A best practice is to bind the runtime variables into the prompt, define a small JSON schema for outputs (scores, rationale) if necessary. This keeps the call deterministic and aligned with governed operations on SAP BTP. Further settings for the AI model regarding filtering, masking, grounding etcetera, have been neglected here.

Within the action's settings, the correct deployment-specific endpoint (see above) must be filled in to address the right deployment model. After that, the action must be released and published in order to use it further in your BPA project.

3.3. Enhanced solution process workflow

After given a high-level overview about how technical model works, I would like to focus on some deeper insights into the automation step “getRequirementDescriptionQualityText” from the workflow solution. In this step, we mainly insert the requirement description and send it to the AI model on SAP AI Core via API. The API provides an evaluation response, which we can use in the following workflow.

This automation keeps it simple: it creates a prompt string, then calls the Orchestrated Completion API to send that prompt to the LLM. The step uses a standard orchestration template, binds the variable myPrompt, and routes the request to the selected model - here, Gemini 2.5 Pro. The orchestration layer returns a structured completion which you can pass to downstream steps.

After we will receive a reply by SAP AI Core via the Orchestration API, the next step in the overall approval workflow is to embed this evaluation in the approval form document, where the author can assess the requirement.

4. Result

Within the SAP BPA Inbox, the respective stakeholders can review the AI-generated evaluation of the requirement and either approve or reject the requirement based on the software quality criteria.

The following image shows the Requirement Approval Form used in our workflow. At the top, the requirement is titled “Customer Order – Order Creation Click & Collect Paid,” followed by a concise business description and prerequisites. Below, standard fields capture the planned completion date and the requester. The lower section highlights the AI‑Powered Evaluation: the requirement is automatically scored against ISO/IEC 25010 criteria (such as completeness and correctness) and accompanied by a clear rationale that summarizes strengths, gaps, and improvement suggestions. This is exactly what approvers see in their task - business context on one side, explainable AI guidance on the other.

The result of the requirement review process will be updated in SAP Cloud ALM accordingly.

5. Conclusion

Aligned with SAP’s AI First strategy, this approach prioritizes trusted, well-governed AI in requirement approvals with minimal effort. It uses clear prompts, standardized orchestration, and flexible model choices to fit different needs. SAP AI First focuses on responsible AI (governance, security, auditability) and on measurable business outcomes, so teams can scale AI in delivery processes with confidence.
 
If you want to take it further from prompt and template design to governance, rollout, and integration in SAP Build Process Automation, my SAP ALM Consulting team can support your end-to-end journey and help you move from pilot to production.

entry.title: Connect SAP OData to Your BPA Excel Forms
entry.content:
Introduction: You're using BPA (SAP Build Process Automation), and users need to fill out Excel files with SAP data. But here's the kicker - to create just one cost center, they have to manually fill 24 rows!
For 10 cost centers? That's  240 rows of mind-numbing data entry! They can't remember all these codes and  this takes more time

Instead of manual data entry, why not connect Excel directly to your SAP OData services? Give them dropdown menus filled with real, live data from your backend!
Prerequisites:
  • OData service
  • SAP system access
  • Excel
Step 1: Open the Gateway to Your Data
Fire up Excel and navigate to: Data → Get Data → From Other Sources → From OData Feed

Step 2: Set Up Your Connection 
When the OData feed dialog opens:
1. Click Advanced 
2. Click the dropdown and select New Parameter

Step 3: Create Your Parameter 
In the parameter setup:
Name: Give it something meaningful like "SAP_System"
Description: SAP System Connection
Type: Select "Any" for flexibility
Suggested Values: Choose "List of values"
 
Add your systems:
  • URL: Your OData service URL
  • Default Value: Your primary system
  • Current Value: What you want to use now

Note:  If you only have one system, skip the advanced stuff and just paste your URL in basic mode

Step 4: Point to Your OData Service
Enter your OData service URL and click OK
This is like giving Excel the address to your data 

Step 5: Authentication Time 
Excel will ask for your SAP credentials:
1. Enter your SAP username and password
2. Select the complete URL you provided
3. Click OK

Step 6: Choose Your Data 
You'll see a screen with checkboxes and multiple tables:
1. Check the boxes for tables you need
2. Look at the Load dropdown at the bottom
3. Select Load to

:377993i0B62606573A70E14:

Step 7: Import Data
1. Select Table
2. Click OK

Each table gets its own worksheet - Excel sheet

Step 8: The Dropdown Data Validation
  1. Select the cell where you want a dropdown
  2. Go to Data → Data Validation

Step 9: Configure Your Dropdown
In the Data Validation screen:
1. Choose List from the dropdown
2. For Source, click the sheet icon
3. Select your data range by selecting the column : `=SheetName!$A:$A`

Step 10: Test Your Creation
Click on your cell and a dropdown appears with real SAP data!

Automatic Data Refresh

To make sure users always see the latest SAP data:

  1. Right‑click on the Query
  2. Select Properties
  3. Enable the checkbox:
    “Refresh data when opening the file”

Now, every time a user opens the Excel file, the data refreshes automatically using their SAP login credentials.

No manual refresh needed. No outdated data. No security risk.

Maintaining SAP System Credentials in Excel (Without Sharing Your Password)

When working with SAP-connected Excel files, one common concern is:
“How do I share this file with users without exposing my SAP username and password?” SAP & Excel already have a secure way to handle this.

How SAP Credentials Are Handled

When you configure an SAP OData connection in Excel (like in Step 3 of your process), it’s natural to wonder whether the file stores your username and password.
But don’t worry — you should never save your credentials inside the file.

Instead, when you’re preparing a template for users (whether through BPA forms, SharePoint, or email), follow this approach:

How to Prepare the Template Securely
  1. You connect the file to the SAP system using your own credentials.
  2. After testing, go to:
    Data → Get Data → Data Source Settings → Edit Permissions → Edit → Basic - remove your username and password 
  3. Save the settings and close the file.

Now, when another user opens the same Excel template:

They will be prompted to enter their own SAP username and password

(Step 5)

This means:

  • You don’t share your credentials
  • They authenticate using their SAP login
  • They only see the data allowed by their SAP roles

Perfectly safe — and no password is stored in the file.

Benefits
  • User-friendly
  • Error reduction
  • Time savings

Conclusion : Users struggle with massive Excel data entry. You've just built a bridge between user-friendly Excel and powerful SAP backends. OData connections turn Excel into a smart frontend and Dropdowns eliminate user errors.

entry.title: ALV with IDA
entry.content:
Mastering SAP ALV with IDA: Applying Record and Column Limits, Select Options, and Disabling Features 

In sap Abap, we can display the data using write statement, using classical reports, using interactive report, using ALV in list and grid format, using Oalv containers, using Salv and         using ALV with Ida (Integrated Data Access). 

Alv with IDA: ALV with IDA (Integrated Data Access) is a modern approach to displaying data in SAP ABAP, leveraging the power of SAP HANA's in-memory capabilities to improve performance and simplify data access. It enables the efficient display of large datasets by pushing down data-intensive operations to the HANA database layer, thereby reducing the data transferred to the application server. 

We can Display the data using the ALV with IDA in sap Gui as well as ADT ( Abap Development Tool). 

 Some Features of Alv with IDA: 

  • In-Memory processing 
  • Code Push Down 
  • Direct Database Access 
  • Automatic performance optimization 
  • Dynamic Data operations like filtering and Sorting 
  • Database level aggregation and calculations 
  • Disabling features like sorting and filtering. 

 Prerequisites for using Alv with IDA: 

  • The SAP system must be running on an SAP HANA database. 
  • The SAP Version should be higher than 7.4. 
  • It is recommended to use latest ADT (Abap Development Tool). 

Standard Classes used: 

cl_salv_gui_table_ida: cl_salv_gui_table_ida is a class in SAP ABAP (Object-Oriented ABAP) that is used to create and display ALV (ABAP List Viewer) grids. The ALV grid provides a powerful and flexible way to display data in a tabular format with various features such as sorting, filtering, restricting the fields and restricting the number of records. 

cl_salv_range_tab_collector: cl_salv_range_tab_collector is a class in SAP ABAP (Object-Oriented ABAP) that is used to provide select options while displaying the data using Alv with IDA.  

Standard Interfaces used: 

if_salv_gui_table_ida - In SAP, the IF_SALV_GUI_TABLE_IDA interface is used for creating and handling ALV (ABAP List Viewer) grids with Integrated Data Access (IDA). 

if_salv_ida_condition_factory - The IF_SALV_IDA_CONDITION_FACTORY interface is used in the context of SAP HANA Integrated Data Access (IDA) for creating conditions and filters on the data being retrieved and displayed in ALV (ABAP List Viewer) grids. 

if_salv_ida_condition - It provides a way to define and manage conditions for filtering, sorting, and aggregating data when working with large datasets in an ALV grid. This interface is particularly useful for specifying conditions to be applied to the data set to retrieve and display relevant data efficiently. 

if_salv_gui_field_catalog_ida - The IF_SALV_GUI_FIELD_CATALOG_IDA interface is used in the context of ALV (ABAP List Viewer) with Integrated Data Access (IDA) to define and manage the field catalog for an ALV grid. The field catalog specifies the properties of the fields (columns) to be displayed in the ALV grid, such as field names, data types, visibility. 

 Standard Exceptions used: 

cx_salv_ida_contract_violation - This exception is raised when there is a violation of a contract or condition defined by the ALV with IDA framework. 

cx_salv_db_connection - The CX_SALV_DB_CONNECTION class is an exception class used to handle database connection errors within the context of the ALV (ABAP List Viewer) with Integrated Data Access (IDA) framework. This exception is specifically designed to manage issues related to the database connection when working with ALV with IDA. 

 cx_salv_db_table_not_supported - The CX_SALV_DB_TABLE_NOT_SUPPORTED exception class is used to handle cases where a database table is not supported by the ALV (ABAP List Viewer) with Integrated Data Access (IDA) framework. This exception is particularly relevant when attempting to use a table that does not meet the requirements or constraints defined by the ALV with IDA framework. 

 cx_salv_ida_contract_violation - The CX_SALV_IDA_CONTRACT_VIOLATION class represents an exception that is used within the ALV (ABAP List Viewer) with Integrated Data Access (IDA) framework to indicate that a contract or condition defined by the framework has been violated. This exception is crucial for maintaining the integrity and expected behavior of the ALV with IDA framework by ensuring that all predefined rules and conditions are met. 

 cx_salv_ida_unknown_name- The CX_SALV_IDA_UNKNOWN_NAME exception class is used within the ALV (ABAP List Viewer) with Integrated Data Access (IDA) framework to indicate that an unknown or invalid name was encountered. This typically refers to situations where a field name, table name, or view name specified in the ALV with IDA framework does not exist or is not recognized. 

 cx_salv_ida_associate_invalid - The CX_SALV_IDA_ASSOCIATE_INVALID exception class is used within the ALV (ABAP List Viewer) with Integrated Data Access (IDA) framework to indicate that an association or relationship between tables or views is invalid. This exception typically arises when there are issues with the definition or use of associations, which are crucial for retrieving and displaying related data in ALV grids. 

 

 What is the Main purpose of this Blog: 

Scenario: I want to display the Material master data using Alv with Ida and restricting the number of records, restring the number of fields, disabling features like sorting, filtering, providing select options and parameters for the Alv. 

I have displayed the data in ADT tool. 

Example1: Displaying Material master data using Alv with Ida 

Output:

 Example2: Displaying the specific number of records using Alv with Ida. 
In this Example we are displaying 10records using Alv with Ida.


Output:

 Example3: Displaying specific fields data only using Alv with Ida. 
In this Example we are displaying specific fields Data using Alv with Ida.
We are deleting the data of fields MBRSH, MTART, ERDAT, ERNAM, ERSDA, Created_at_time

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& Report ztk_alv_restricting_fields 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
REPORT ztk_alv_restricting_fields. 

data:lr_salv TYPE Ref TO if_salv_gui_table_ida, 
     lr_fcat TYPE REF TO if_salv_gui_field_catalog_ida. 

try. 
cl_salv_gui_table_ida=>create( 
                         EXPORTING 
                           iv_table_name = 'MARA'  
                         RECEIVING 
                          ro_alv_gui_table_ida = lr_salv 
). 
CATCH cx_salv_db_connection. 
CATCH cx_salv_db_table_not_supported. 
CATCH cx_salv_ida_contract_violation. 
ENDTRY. 

* get the field catalog reference 
lr_fcat = lr_salv->field_catalog( ). 

* get all the fields 
lr_fcat->get_available_fields( 
                IMPORTING 
                ets_field_names = data(lt_field) 
). 

* Delete few fields as needed 
loop at lt_field INTO DATA(ls_Wa). 
if ls_wa = 'MBRSH' OR LS_WA = 'MTART' OR LS_wA = 'ERDAT' OR LS_wA = 'ERNAM' 
OR LS_wA = 'CREATED_AT_TIME' OR LS_wA = 'ERSDA'.

DELETE LT_FIELD INDEX sy-tabix. 

ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 

*SET back the fields to be displayed 
try. 
lr_fcat->set_available_fields( its_field_names = lt_field ). 
*CATCH cx_salv_ida_unknown_name. 
ENDTRY. 

* set maximum rows to display 
TRy. 
lr_salv->set_maximum_number_of_rows( 
                  EXPORTING 
                       iv_number_of_rows = 10 
). 
CATCH cx_salv_param_out_of_bounds. 
ENDTRY. 

* Display alv 

lr_salv->fullscreen( )->display( ). 
*CATCH cx_salv_ida_contract_violation.

Output

 Example: Displaying the data based on parameter using Alv with Ida.

In This Example we are Displaying the Data based on Parameter using Alv with Ida.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& Report ztk_alv_parameter 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
REPORT ztk_alv_parameter. 

PARAMETERS: p_MTART TYPE MARA-MTART. 
DATA: lr_salv TYPE REF TO if_salv_gui_table_ida, 
      lr_cond_fact TYPE REF TO if_salv_ida_condition_factory, 
      lr_cond TYPE REF TO if_salv_ida_condition. 

CL_SALV_GUI_TABLE_IDA=>create( 
                  EXPORTING 
                      iv_table_name = 'MARA' 
                  RECEIVING 
                     ro_alv_gui_table_ida = LR_SALV 
). 
* CATCH cx_salv_db_connection. 
* CATCH cx_salv_db_table_not_supported. 
* CATCH cx_salv_ida_contract_violation. 

LR_COND_FACT = LR_sALV->condition_factory( ). 
LR_COND = LR_COND_FACT->equals( 
                          name = 'MTART' 
                          value = P_MTART ). 
* CATCH cx_salv_ida_unknown_name. 
LR_sALV->set_select_options( 
                   EXPORTING 
                      io_condition = LR_COND ). 
*CATCH cx_salv_ida_associate_invalid. 
*CATCH cx_salv_db_connection. 
*CATCH cx_salv_ida_condition_invalid. 
*CATCH cx_salv_ida_unknown_name. 

LR_sALV->fullscreen( )->display( ). 

*CATCH cx_salv_ida_contract_violation.

Output

Example: Displaying the data based on select-options using Alv with Ida .

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& Report ztk_alv_selectopt 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
REPORT ztk_alv_selectopt. 

DATA: lv_MATNR TYPE MARA-MATNR. 
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_MATNR FOR lv_MATNR. 
DATA: lr_salv TYPE REF TO if_salv_gui_table_ida, 
      lr_range TYPE REF TO cl_salv_range_tab_collector. 

cl_salv_gui_table_ida=>create( 
                         EXPORTING 
                             iv_table_name = 'MARA' 
                          RECEIVING 
                              ro_alv_gui_table_ida = lr_salv ). 

CREATE OBJECT lr_range. 

*Set the range 
lr_range->add_ranges_for_name( iv_name = 'MATNR' it_ranges = s_MATNR[] ). 
lr_range->get_collected_ranges( IMPORTING et_named_ranges = DATA(lt_ranges) ). 

lr_salv->set_select_options( 
                   EXPORTING 
                     it_ranges = lt_ranges ). 

* Display ALV 
lr_salv->fullscreen( )->display( ).

Output

 

Example 6: Displaying the data in zebra pattern and providing title using Alv with Ida.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& Report ztk_alv_title_zebrapattern 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
REPORT ztk_alv_title_zebrapattern. 

DATA: lr_salv TYPE REF TO if_salv_gui_table_ida, 
      lr_disp TYPE REF TO if_salv_gui_table_display_opt. 

cl_salv_gui_table_ida=>create( 
                          EXPORTING 
                            iv_table_name = 'MARA' 
                          RECEIVING 
                            ro_alv_gui_table_ida = lr_salv ). 

* Get display option reference 
lr_disp = lr_salv->display_options( ). 

* Set the title 
lr_disp->set_title( iv_title = 'Material Master' ). 

* Set zebra pattern 
lr_disp->enable_alternating_row_pattern( ). 

* Display ALV 
lr_salv->fullscreen( )->display( ).

Output:

Example: Displaying the data using ALV with Ida and disabling features like sorting, filtering for a particular field   

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& Report ztk_alv_disable_arithmatic 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
REPORT ztk_alv_disable_arithmatic. 

data:lr_salv TYPE REF TO if_Salv_gui_table_ida, 
     lt_fcat TYPE REF TO if_salv_gui_field_catalog_ida. 

try. 
cl_salv_gui_table_ida=>create( 
                         EXPORTING 
                            iv_table_name = 'MARA' 
                         RECEIVING 
                            ro_alv_gui_table_ida = lr_salv 
). 
* CATCH cx_salv_db_connection. 
* CATCH cx_salv_db_table_not_supported. 
* CATCH cx_salv_ida_contract_violation. 
ENDTRY. 

lt_fcat = lr_Salv->field_catalog( ). 
lt_fcat->disable_filter( iv_field_name = 'MTART' ). 
LT_FCAT->disable_sort( iv_field_name = 'MTART' ).

* CATCH cx_salv_ida_unknown_name. 
* CATCH cx_salv_call_after_1st_display. 
* CATCH cx_salv_ida_unknown_name. 
* CATCH cx_salv_call_after_1st_display.

lr_salv->fullscreen( )->display( ).
* CATCH cx_salv_ida_contract_violation.

Output

Here i have disabled sorting and filtering for material type (MTART).

For remaining fields,the features like sorting and filtering are enabled 

Conclusion:

In this Blog, we  explored how to display the data using ALV with IDA (Integrated Data Access) in SAP ABAP, leveraging the power of SAP HANA's in-memory capabilities to improve performance and simplify data access. It enables the efficient display of large datasets by pushing down data-intensive operations to the HANA database layer, thereby reducing the data transferred to the application server. We explored how to disable filtering and sorting for a particular field in ALV,
how to display data using ALV with IDA in zebra pattern, Displayed Data with parameters and select options, displaying specific number of records, Displaying specific fields Data.

Thanks.

entry.title: Unlock Continuous Proficiency: Introducing the WalkMe Learning Arc Learning Journey
entry.content:
In today’s fast‑moving digital landscape, organizations can no longer rely on traditional, portal‑based training to keep employees effective, confident, and compliant. Skills decay quickly. Applications evolve constantly. And enterprises need learning that keeps pace.

That’s why we’re excited to introduce the WalkMe Learning Arc Learning Journey—a complete curriculum designed to empower partners and customers with the knowledge, confidence, and positioning expertise to bring AI‑native, in‑the‑flow learning to their organizations and clients.

Why WalkMe Learning Arc?

WalkMe Learning Arc is not another LMS. It is digital learning delivered directly inside live applications, meeting users in the exact moment of need. Combined with the WalkMe Digital Adoption Platform (DAP), it creates a seamless ecosystem that connects training (knowing) with action (doing)—closing the final mile of workforce readiness.

With AI-assisted authoring, targeted delivery, and unified insights, WalkMe Learning Arc helps organizations achieve:

  • Faster time to proficiency
  • Higher adoption of new processes & programs
  • Reduced errors and risk
  • A modern, engaging employee experience

WalkMe Learning Arc also provides a strategic path for SAP customers transitioning from SAP Enable Now, offering content preservation and modernization while reducing operational friction.

About the Learning Journey

This three‑course journey equips you to discover, position, and sell WalkMe Learning Arc with clarity and confidence:

1. Discovering WalkMe Learning Arc

Gain a high‑level understanding of WMLA’s capabilities, AI-native design, and how it integrates with WalkMe DAP to deliver actionable, in‑flow learning. Learn how WMLA transforms training from passive, portal‑based modules to proactive, targeted support within actual workflows.

2. Positioning WalkMe Learning Arc

Learn how to articulate the value of WMLA—credibly, simply, and in alignment with SAP standards. Master the core value drivers (proficiency, productivity, risk reduction, and employee experience) and learn how to map customer challenges to measurable outcomes.

3. Selling WalkMe Learning Arc

Perfect for customer-facing teams, this course helps you identify ideal customer profiles, lead SAP Enable Now transition conversations, overcome common objections, and deliver compelling 30- and 90‑second pitches grounded in business impact.

Who Is This For?

  • SAP partners and consultants supporting S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, or Ariba programs
  • WalkMe partners expanding into learning in the flow of work
  • Customers modernizing training operations or transitioning from SAP Enable Now
  • Change management, L&D, DAP, and digital adoption leaders

If you influence workforce enablement or digital transformation, this Learning Journey is designed for you.

Why This Matters Now

WalkMe Learning Arc offers a modern, proven path forward.

The Learning Journey equips you with the insights, messaging, and confidence to guide your organization—or your customers—through that transformation.

Start Your Learning Journey Today

Whether you're helping customers modernize their training ecosystem or looking to elevate your own organization’s proficiency strategy, the WalkMe Learning Arc Learning Journey gives you everything you need to lead with impact.

Discover it. Position it. Sell it.
